answer:
We are asked for the additional information (in bits) when learning a blue-eyed girl is also blonde.  
First, find \( P(\text{blonde} \mid \text{blue eyes}) \).  
Let \( B \) = blonde, \( E \) = blue eyes. Given: \( P(B) = 0.25 \), \( P(E) = 0.5 \), and \( P(E \mid B) = 0.75 \).  
Then \( P(B \cap E) = P(B)P(E \mid B) = 0.25 \times 0.75 = 0.1875 \).  
So \( P(B \mid E) = \frac{0.1875}{0.5} = 0.375 \).  

The additional information is the self-information:  
\( I = -\log_2 P(B \mid E) = -\log_2(0.375) \approx
